risinfo.chaptertitle: 16. Noncritical Hospital Evaluation and Management Services

sect4.title: AAP Coding Assistance and Education
sect4.para: Pediatric Evaluation and Management: Coding Quick Reference Card 2026 (www.aap.org/Pediatric-Evaluation-and-Management-Coding-Quick-Reference-Card-2026) Pediatric Evaluation and Management Coding Revisions: Facility and Office Services (www.aap.org/pediatric-evaluation-and-management-coding-revisions-facility-and-office-services-paperback)

sect4.title: AAP Pediatric Coding Newsletter
sect4.para: "Coding Challenge: Hospital Evaluation and Management Services," March 2024 (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book239 document003) "Coding Challenge Answers: Hospital Evaluation and Management Services," March 2024 (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book239 document006)

"Coding Review: Social Determinants of Health," November 2023 (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book235 document006) "CPT 2024: New Guidelines for Brief Hospital Stays," November 2023 (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book235 document004)

"CPT 2024: Revisions to Guidelines for Split or Shared Visits," November 2023 (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book235 document003) "Endocrinology: Updated ICD-10-CM Diabetes and Hypoglycemia Codes," October 2024 (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book246 document001)

"You Code It! Adolescent Weight Loss Due to a Suspected Eating Disorder," October 2024 (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book246 document005) "You Code It! Answers: Adolescent Weight Loss Due to a Suspected Eating Disorder," October 2024 (https://doi.org/10.1542/pcco book246 document007)

listitem.para: indicates a new code; ▲, revised; †, re-sequenced; ✚, add-on; ✱, modifier 51 exempt; ♦, audiovisual technology; and ♠, synchronous interactive audio.

sect4.para: "You Code It! Stage 2 Hypertension, Hematuria, and Edema," May 2024 (https://doi.org/10.1542/peco book241 document002) "You Code It! Answers: Stage 2 Hypertension, Hematuria, and Edema," May 2024 (https://doi.org/10.1542/peco book241 document004)

para.emphasis: True or false? Because the codes for inpatient and observation E/M services are the same, it is not necessary for a physician or QHP to document a change in admission status.

Which type of hospital E/M service is reported by a physician or QHP providing inpatient care to a patient who has received services from a physician of the same exact specialty and subspecialty and same group practice on a prior date during the current stay?

listitem.para: Initial hospital inpatient or observation services
Any of the above, but the code is appended with modifier FS

para.emphasis: What type of code is reported for hospital care when the physician who ordered the admission provides initial care and discharge management at the same time (ie, at a single visit)?

listitem.para: Initial hospital care and hospital discharge day management

para.emphasis: Which of the following is a consultation?

listitem.para: A physician or QHP provides an initial E/M service to a child whose physician requested advice on management of an infection by anunidentified agent.
A physician or QHP provides a follow-up visit to a child whose test results indicate need for additional advice or opinion on management.

A QHP requests that a physician who has provided outpatient behavioral health care to a hospitalized patient assume inpatient management of the patient's behavioral health needs during an admission for an unrelated condition.
A physician provides continuing care to a patient while covering for the patient's physician who is of the same specialty and same group practice.

para.emphasis: Which is true of prolonged service code 99418?

listitem.para: 99418 is reported for each additional hour beyond the total time required to report the related E/M service.
99418 is reported in addition to any hospital inpatient or observation code when the physician's total time exceeds that required for the primary E/M service.
99418 is reported only when the primary E/M service code is selected on the basis of total time and the total time is exceeded by at least 15 minutes.
99418 is reported for prolonged service within 24 hours of the primary E/M service.
